context_lines: In **Matthew 27**, Jesus suffers, is crucified, and dies under the weight of redemptive judgment. The chapter's cry from the cross must be read in the larger scriptural and covenantal frame of the passion, not as a denial of the Father's plan.
explanation_lines: This is the rawest prayer in the Bible. Jesus is not asking God to stop what is happening. He is not requesting relief. He is bringing the full, unfiltered weight of what He is experiencing into words, directed at the Father, in the darkest moment any human being has ever faced. The Father does not answer immediately. He does not remove the suffering. The cup is drunk to the last drop. If Jesus could bring this kind of honesty before God without it being unfaithful, so can we.
